rulings.Case Title: HAROLD BRANDEL VS. RONALD C SILVERSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 29, 2019, Line 13, PETITIONER HAROLD BRANDEL's Motion On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Petitioners Harold Brandel, Ted Vinther, Walter Wright, and Gabriel Stern's petition to confirm the Final Arbitration Award is granted in part and denied in part. The Final Award is vacated only to the extent that it declares that the Sublease between Thirsty Bear Brewing Company LLC (TBLLC) and non-party California Craft Maltings LLC dba Admiral Maltings (CCM) is of no force and effect, and is confirmed in all other respects. A draft order containing the Court's full tentative ruling will be provided to counsel prior to or at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN NEALAN VS. LEDCOR CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Augment Expert Witness Disclosure
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 29, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT LEDCOR CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s Motion To Augment Expert Witness Disclosure. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JACK H REISNER VS. UNITED BROTHERHOOD OF CARPENTERS AND JOINERS OF AM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions, Including Terminating Or Evidence Sanctions, And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 29, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT UNITED BROTHERHOOD OF CARPENTERS NATIONAL JOB CORPS TRAINING FUND, INC.'s Amended Motion For Sanctions, Including Terminating Or Evidence Sanctions, And Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. Judge Schulman granted Defendant's motion for summary judgment on August 15, 2019, therefore, this motion is moot. Moreover, assuming arguendo that the motion for summary judgment had been denied, while certain discovery issues potentially remain that could be the subject of further motion practice, the conduct of Plaintiff does not arise to that which would justify the granting of terminating sanctions at this juncture. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHANNA WELTY VS. CRAIG W. MORRISON
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 29, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT CRAIG MORRISON's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustain without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER ORAZIO VS. CAMBRIA COMPANY LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 29, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF JENNIFER ORAZIO's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint. In this employment discrimination action, plaintiff Jennifer Orazio seeks to state a number of causes of action against her former employer and supervisor, including claims for sexual harassment, assault and battery, and sexual battery. In her original unverified complaint, filed on November 29, 2017, plaintiff alleged that throughout her employment from June 2014 until she was terminated on March 30, 2017, her supervisor, defendant John Brekke, subjected her to unwelcome physical contact, including hugging, kissing, touching, and sexual suggestive communications and contact. (Complaint, 7.) She alleged this harassment began at a Christmas party in December 2014, and that the "most recent incident [of harassment] occurred in February 2017 during the Cambria National Sales meeting in Palm Springs, California," when she alleges Brekke "again approached Jennifer on the dance floor while intoxicated and grabbed her." (Id. 18.) She repeated the same allegation, verbatim, in her first amended complaint, filed on October 22, 2018. (FAC, 18.) On March 14, 2019, plaintiff sought leave to file a second amended complaint to add and remove certain claims, and "to add facts supporting all of [plaintiff's] claims . . . and to correct some allegations." In particular, plaintiff sought leave to correct the quoted allegation and instead to allege that "the most recent incident occurred in February 2016" during the company national sales meeting. (SAC, 18.) The court granted the motion on certain conditions. The Court's full tentative ruling is emailed to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
